DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED IS CONTINUED TO MAY 13, 2014 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 29, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. THE SUBJECT LINE OF THE EMAIL SHALL INCLUDE THE LINE NUMBER, CASE NAME AND CASE NUMBER. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.=(302/JPT)

Off Calendar May-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jill Bates-Moore, Esq of Your Community Lawyer (510-985-9250) for Unifund, CCR LLC, Plaintiff and moving party arrived at 10:20 am.
No other appearance on the matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC's, Motion For Order To Deem Matters Admitted is granted as unopposed. Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details) Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Continued To May-13-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR LLC's Motion For Order To Deem Matters Admitted is Continued to May 13, 2014 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 29, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)
